name: Grade 1 Language Arts — Semester 2
description: An 18-week Grade 1 pathway for opinion and informational writing, research, revision, and presentation. Learning builds independence through investigation, guided practice, application, and reflection.
learningGoals: Develop first-grade knowledge and skills in opinion and informational writing, research, revision, and presentation.
Use speaking, drawing, writing, numbers, models, or movement to explain thinking.
Work independently when ready and ask for help, feedback, or clarification when needed.

lessons.title: Explore opinion and informational writing, research, revision, and presentation
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record opinion and informational writing, research, revision, and presentation by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record opinion and informational writing, research, revision, and presentation by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

resources.title: Common Core State Standards for ELA
resources.description: Planning reference for this Grade 1 Language Arts pathway.

units.title: Investigate: opinion and informational writing, research, revision, and presentation
units.essentialQuestion: What evidence can we gather about opinion and informational writing, research, revision, and presentation?
outcomes: I can observe, discuss, and record opinion and informational writing, research, revision, and presentation.
I can use accurate words, pictures, numbers, models, writing, or movement to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Explore opinion and informational writing, research, revision, and presentation
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record opinion and informational writing, research, revision, and presentation by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

activities.title: Notice and investigate
activities.learnerAction: The learner uses age-appropriate materials and first-grade vocabulary to observe, discuss, and record opinion and informational writing, research, revision, and presentation. Adult supervision is provided whenever the subject involves tools, heat, animals, water, traffic, or online media.

successCriteria: The learner completes a first-grade task connected to opinion and informational writing, research, revision, and presentation.
The learner explains an observation, choice, or strategy with appropriate support.
The learner follows safety, care, and collaboration expectations.
